title: Writable Layers, Volumes, Bind Mounts, tmpfs, Ownership, and Recovery · Docker Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares fresh writable layers, volumes, bind mounts, tmpfs, ownership, and recovery decisions and accepts only answers tied to observable image, container, build, or Compose evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for storage selection, ownership, backup, and restore plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain immutable image layers, container writable-layer lifetime, copy-on-write costs, and containerd image-store versus classic-driver boundaries.
Create, inspect, mount, share, label, back up, restore, migrate, and remove named and anonymous volumes with explicit ownership.
Use bind mounts with deliberate host path, existence, read-only mode, propagation, SELinux, UID/GID, and portability boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Docker Storage Backup Restore Test
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz database writes into an anonymous volume while developers rely on a host-path bind mount. A backup archive exists but has never restored. Define ownership, consistency, lifecycle, checksum, and clean-volume recovery. Competency probe: Quiesce or snapshot consistently, create a bounded backup, verify integrity, restore into a fresh volume, and rehearse application recovery.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Docker Storage Backup Restore Test.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for quiesce or snapshot consistently, create a bounded backup, verify integrity, restore into a fresh volume, and rehearse application recovery. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Docker Storage Backup Restore Test, keep Docker Storage Backup Restore Test connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz database writes into an anonymous volume while developers rely on a host-path bind mount. A backup archive exists but has never restored. Define ownership, consistency, lifecycle, checksum, and clean-volume recovery. Competency probe: Quiesce or snapshot consistently, create a bounded backup, verify integrity, restore into a fresh volume, and rehearse application recovery.
